pulling ears, BF
Immunization: Mmmr,varicella and Hep A, if pt is doing well and if okay with the provider.E BF
HPI: Patient has been coughing for the past 2 days along with runny nose. He vomited once last night
after finishing a bottle of milk before going to sleep. No blood in vomit. No diarrhea. Denies fever. Patient
consistently tugs on his ears and has history of recurrent ear infections.
Patient has had 4 episodes of recurrent bilateral/right otitis media:
7/15/2025: tx with Augmentin
7/26/2025: tx with Augmentin (Urgent care- thought that previous tx was with Amoxicillin, but it was
Augmentin)
10/08/2025 : tx with Amoxcillin (right AOM)
11/12/2025 (today): tx with Cefdinir

CHEST/LUNGS: symmetric expansion, no subcostal retractions or tracheal tugging, clear to auscultation

ABDOMEN: normoactive bowel sounds, soft, non-tender, non-distended, no mass or organomegaly
EXTREMITIES: warm and well perfused, capillary refill < 2 seconds
NEURO: normal strength and tone

No results found for this or any previous visit (from the past 12 hours).

claude_vision.referral_reason: Recurrent acute suppurative otitis media (bilateral) — referred to ENT for evaluation and consideration of PE (pressure equalization) tube placement due to 4 documented episodes of bilateral/right acute otitis media within approximately 4 months (July–November 2025).
relevant_clinical_findings: 4 episodes of recurrent bilateral/right otitis media within ~4 months: 7/15/2025 (Augmentin), 7/26/2025 (Augmentin), 10/08/2025 (Amoxicillin, right AOM), 11/12/2025 (Cefdinir, today's visit)
Physical exam on 11/12/2025: Bilateral TM with erythema and purulent effusion (bolded in note)
Nasal congestion with copious clear rhinorrhea; oropharynx with mild erythema
Patient consistently tugs on ears; ear pain reported by parent
Current symptoms: cough x2 days, runny nose x1 day, vomiting started the day before visit
Vitals: Pulse 121, Resp 33, Temp 98.6°F (afebrile), SpO2 98%, Weight 10.8 kg (pediatric patient)
No past medical history on file
Hearing test noted as required prior to ENT visit; two booths required per scheduling note from Lee, Caroline on 11/17/2025
missing_information: No audiology/hearing test results included — scheduling note from 11/17/2025 states 'Hearing test is needed first; two booths required'; hearing evaluation was completed 2/10/2026 per scheduling records but the actual audiogram/results report is not attached to this referral packet
No prior ENT notes or specialist evaluations attached
Referring physician listed as 'Saied, Rahat, MD' in reason for visit but the referral order was placed by 'Garello, Morgan Drew, NP' — no direct contact information for referring provider included
Patient name and DOB are redacted/illegible in this copy, limiting verification
Nystatin (MYCOSTATIN) prescription dated 9/25/2024 with end date 2/10/2026 is listed in the medication list with no associated diagnosis or clinical context provided

provider_urgency_label.label: urgent
provider_urgency_label.source: Multiple sources: (1) Referral notes page 1 — attachment label reads 'URG' (Document on 12/3/2025 1018 by Herrera-Vega, Cecilia: URG); (2) Email subject line visible in 12/02/2025 scheduling note reads 'RE: Urgent Referral - EXPO'; (3) Referral Information section (page 4) — Priority field is listed as 'Urgent'

criteria_check.provider_label: Urgent — documented in the Priority field ('Priority: Urgent'), the scheduling message subject line ('RE: Urgent Referral - EXPO'), and the attachment label ('URG') by Herrera-Vega, Cecilia on 12/3/2025
criteria_check.reasoning: The referring provider marked this referral as urgent across multiple points in the document, but the clinical content — recurrent otitis media with 4 episodes over 4 months and bilateral TM effusion — does not match any of the defined ENT urgent criteria (no sudden hearing loss, no airway compromise, no malignancy, no peritonsillar abscess, no foreign body, no acute nasal fracture within window, no tongue tie with feeding issues); therefore, secondary review is required to determine whether the provider has additional clinical context justifying urgent scheduling or whether the urgency label should be downgraded.

claude_vision.next_steps: Secondary review needed — provider marked this referral urgent (confirmed in Priority field, scheduling message, and attachment label), but the clinical content (recurrent otitis media, 4 episodes) does not match any defined ENT urgent criteria; triage team should confirm with referring provider whether additional clinical concern (e.g., suspected hearing loss, failed hearing screen) justifies urgent scheduling before proceeding — and note that a hearing evaluation (two booths required) must be completed first, with the ENT new patient consultation scheduled approximately 15 minutes after.
claude_vision.summary: This is a pediatric referral to ENT (Sacramento Ear, Nose & Throat) for a young child with 4 documented episodes of recurrent acute bilateral otitis media between July and November 2025, with bilateral TM erythema and purulent effusion found on exam; the referring NP is requesting evaluation for possible PE tube placement. The referral was authorized, the hearing test and ENT new patient consultation were both completed on 2/10/2026. The provider labeled this referral as urgent, but the clinical content does not meet defined urgent ENT criteria, and the hearing evaluation results are not included in this packet — secondary review is recommended to confirm appropriateness of the urgency designation.
